title: malva × zoernigii
intro: Malva × zoernigii, also known as the Mallow or Cheeseweed, is a hybrid plant species within the Mallow family, characterized by its distinctive white flowers and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Malva × zoernigii include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Malva × zoernigii can be prepared as a tea, poultice, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
